title: [FIRRTL] instance invalid bindings
body:
The following FIRRTL program

```python
circuit top_mod :
  module mod_0 :
    input tmp1: UInt<5>
    output tmp2: UInt<5>
    tmp2 <= tmp1
  module top_mod :
    input tmp1: UInt<5>
    output tmp2: UInt<5>
    inst U0 of mod_0
    U0 is invalid
```

Compiled with `firtool --lower-to-rtl` gives an error:

```mlir
a_top_mod.fir:10:8: error: expected '.' in field reference
    U0 is invalid
       ^
```

Compiled with `firrtl-1.5.0` produces this Verilog:

```verilog
module mod_0(
  input  [4:0] tmp1,
  output [4:0] tmp2
);
  assign tmp2 = tmp1;
endmodule
module top_mod(
  input  [4:0] tmp1,
  output [4:0] tmp2
);
  wire [4:0] U0_tmp1;
  wire [4:0] U0_tmp2;
  mod_0 U0 (
    .tmp1(U0_tmp1),
    .tmp2(U0_tmp2)
  );
  assign tmp2 = U0_tmp2;
  assign U0_tmp1 = tmp1;
endmodule
```
